task_completion.description: Verify that the absolute difference image FITS file is saved in the correct results directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal listing showing 'difference_image.fits' inside the '~/AstroImages/cosmic_ray_project/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the cosmic ray report text file is created in the results directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal listing showing 'cosmic_ray_report.txt' inside the '~/AstroImages/cosmic_ray_project/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the report contains the basic image statistics: Exposure time and Image dimensions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'cosmic_ray_report.txt' displayed in a text editor or terminal (via cat/less), showing labeled values for 'Exposure time' and 'Image dimensions'.

task_completion.description: Verify the report contains the difference image statistics: Median, Standard deviation, and Threshold value used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'cosmic_ray_report.txt' showing labeled values for 'Difference image median', 'Difference image standard deviation', and 'Threshold value used'.

task_completion.description: Verify the report contains the final cosmic ray calculations: Number of events detected and Hit rate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'cosmic_ray_report.txt' showing labeled values for 'Number of cosmic ray events detected' and 'Cosmic ray hit rate (events/pixel/second)'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used a script or appropriate software to process the FITS files rather than fabricating the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A terminal showing the execution of a Python script, an open Jupyter notebook, or an interactive Python session importing libraries like 'astropy' and 'numpy'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually performed the required mathematical operations (absolute difference, median, std dev, 5-sigma threshold).
integrity.visual_evidence: Code snippets visible in an editor or terminal showing operations like 'numpy.abs(data1 - data2)', 'numpy.median', 'numpy.std', and a threshold calculation multiplying the std dev by 5.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply hardcode a pre-written text file without reading the source FITS files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's code should show dynamic extraction of the exposure time from the FITS header (e.g., 'header["EXPTIME"]') and dynamic calculation of the array dimensions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The data consists of real cryogenically cooled CCD dark frames from the Large Format Camera (LFC) on the Palomar 200-inch Hale Telescope (Astropy CCD Reduction Guide dataset). The agent must explicitly use a sigma threshold of 5 to calculate the cosmic ray events.
